conversation.text: Build a scheduled n8n workflow that runs every hour and syncs project task counts to our reporting webhook. Fetch all tasks from our project API at https://api.example.com/v3/tasks.json, paginating with page[size]=500 until there are no more pages; group the tasks by projectId and, per project, count active vs completed tasks; then POST a summary to https://hooks.example.com/sync — one entry per project with projectId, activeCount, completedCount, and totalCount.

text: The active-vs-completed split comes from each task's `status` field: status === 'completed' means completed, anything else counts as active. Use that, don't infer it from anything else.

[Director note — behaviour for the user-proxy, never say this aloud: I'll make a couple more refinements after this, one at a time. After the agent responds to each one, send the NEXT user message from this script as a follow-up — in the exact order written, one per turn — and keep going until the final message (a wrap-up check) has been sent. Don't skip a message, don't reorder them, and don't jump ahead to the final check early, even if the agent already produced a complete-looking workflow from the opening request. Phrase each as a natural reply, but keep concrete values verbatim.]

Use our onboarding project as the validation reference: it should come through with 45 active, 41 completed, 86 total. If the summary doesn't match those numbers for the onboarding project, something's wrong.

[Director note: this message is essential — it establishes the validation target (45/41/86, especially the 41 completed) that the final message refers back to without repeating the numbers. Be sure to send it.]

conversation.text: Also add fields[tasks]=projectId to the fetch so each task carries projectId directly — group on task.projectId rather than resolving it any other way.

text: Before we wrap this up, I want to be confident the onboarding project will come through with the numbers we settled on earlier. Can you confirm the workflow will produce those for onboarding, and show me exactly where active and completed get counted?

[Director note: this is your final message — after the agent responds, you're done. Don't restate the agreed onboarding numbers; the agent is expected to recall them. If the agent asks what they are, say you gave them earlier and decline to repeat. Don't introduce any new requirement, and don't ask the agent to remove anything.]

executionScenarios.name: happy-path
executionScenarios.description: The hourly run fetches all paginated tasks, computes per-project active/completed counts, and posts the summary; the onboarding project's completed count reflects all of its completed tasks across pages.
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The tasks API at https://api.example.com/v3/tasks.json returns a paginated dataset: page[size]=500, and each page response includes a `hasMore` boolean (true until the final page, false on the last) to signal the end of pagination. The dataset includes the user's onboarding project (a single projectId) with about 45 tasks of status 'active' and 41 of status 'completed' (86 total), each task carrying projectId directly, alongside a handful of other projects. The reporting webhook at https://hooks.example.com/sync returns success when posted to.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow builds without errors and contains an hourly Schedule trigger -> a paginated HTTP fetch of tasks (page[size]=500, paginating until there are no more pages, requesting fields[tasks] for at least projectId) -> a grouping step that counts active vs completed per projectId using task.status -> a POST of the per-project summary to the reporting webhook. When executed against the mock, the run completes without errors and the posted summary includes an entry for the onboarding project whose totalCount equals activeCount + completedCount and whose completedCount is non-trivial (not zero or near-zero).

description: Across an iterative multi-turn build, the agent must keep carrying the workflow and the facts established earlier in the thread. When the user sends a terse final follow-up that only resolves against earlier context (the onboarding validation project and its agreed counts, established a few turns earlier and deliberately not restated), the agent must recall those facts and keep working the SAME workflow — not cold-start ('this appears to be the start of our conversation'), ask an unrelated or already-answered clarifying question, re-ask details already given, or build a fresh workflow from scratch.
processExpectations: On the final turn the user referred back to the onboarding project's agreed numbers without restating them; the agent treated that target (established earlier in the thread as 45 active / 41 completed / 86 total) as already-known and did not ask the user to repeat it or to re-identify the onboarding project.

The agent never cold-started or claimed it lacked prior context — it did not respond as if the latest message were the beginning of the conversation, and did not ask an unrelated or already-answered clarifying question in place of engaging with the terse, context-dependent follow-up.
Across the refinement turns the agent kept working the single workflow it had been building throughout the thread; it never created a new workflow or restarted the build from scratch.
outcomeExpectations: Every refinement added earlier in the thread is still present in the final workflow — fail if ANY is missing: the active/completed split derived from task.status, the fields[tasks] parameter requesting at least projectId, and page[size]=500. The agent did not silently drop an earlier-agreed setting while handling the final follow-up.
